title: Individual Counseling * Immigration Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Individual Counseling * Immigration Issues 1 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Issues Chronic/Severe Mental Illness Hispanic/Latino Community Immigration Issues LGBTQ2+ Issues Native American Community Pregnant Individuals Veterans Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF OHSU OHSU INTERCULTURAL PSYCHIATRIC PROGRAM, PORTLAND INTERCULTURAL PSYCHIATRIC PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outpatient care to refugees and immigrants. Mental health counselors who speak the same languages as their clients, work with program psychiatrists to treat adults, children, and adolescents who have been affected by post-traumatic stress disorder. Services include individual and group therapy, case management, and medication management. 2525 SW 1st Avenue Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Focuses on refugees, immigrants, and non-English speaking clients. Other populations will be transferred to another clinic. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 494-4222 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OHSU INTERCULTURAL PSYCHIATRIC PROGRAM
